ending: Ruwen's party defeats the skeleton army occupying the ruined city, although the wounded bone sculptor escapes by teleportation. Inside the third temple, Ruwen and Hamma discover more than 700,000 dead awaiting revival, including Ruwen's parents and Hamma's father. Uru passes Architect's divine authority to Ruwen. Through its Navigator branch, he produces the required location keys and timing data. His soulbound adviser then creates a channel to the temple intelligence, allowing restoration to continue. The temple guardians awaken, Uru's blessing spreads across the region, and the three family members enter revival baths with two days remaining before their return. Ruwen, Hamma, Sift, the restored shade companion, Shelly, Whiskers, and Smash survive. Ruwen must now build civic and military councils, appoint a high priest, restore the city, defend Uru's southern border, manage the immense revival queue, and conceal the true reach of his powers. Blapy arrives with allies and asks where to establish a portal linking the temple-city to Fractal, leaving that connection as Ruwen's immediate decision.
in_short: Stranded in the spirit realm, Ruwen learns to cultivate and cast with spirit, rescues Uru's lost champions, defeats a plague siren, and survives a tribunal duel while concealing his extraordinary twelve-meridian core. He helps Sift form a path to cultivation and opens the Iris portal, bringing the party home. Uru then sends Ruwen, Hamma, Sift, and their companions to restore her ruined third temple before war reaches the southern border. They defeat its skeleton army, though the bone sculptor escapes, and discover a revival queue holding more than 700,000 dead, including Ruwen's parents and Hamma's father. Uru gives Ruwen the divine Architect role, enabling him to restart the temple. Its guardians awaken, the three relatives begin a two-day revival, and Blapy arrives seeking a portal site connecting the restored city to Fractal.

recaps.text: Previously: Ruwen Starfield ascended as one of Uru's champions in Deepwell and became entangled in a conflict involving the hostile deity Naktos. He formed close bonds with Hamma and Sift, gained the Root class alongside his Worker path, and acquired unusual harvesting abilities that could draw divine attention. He also became Fractal's keeper, creating a continuing duty to the young sentient dungeon. Tremine, once a learned ally, betrayed Ruwen to Naktos and helped strand Ruwen's party in the spirit realm. Ruwen's parents remain missing, and Naktos' agents and invasion plans continue to threaten Uru's lands.

So far this book: Ruwen's group is trapped in Fractal's spirit-realm dungeon, where fatal wounds heal slowly instead of ending consciousness and ordinary interfaces and magic do not work. Ruwen nevertheless rescues a hostile house captain and former bully who had tried to kill him. He, Sift, and the restored shade defeat a spirit-using creature, although Sift loses an arm. By sharing spirit and teaching Sift a breathing method, Ruwen helps reconnect the limb. Fractal guides everyone through a submerged passage toward the mines, forcing them to endure repeated drowning pain. In a hidden cavern, Ruwen condenses his spirit into an infinity-shaped core and reaches cultivation stage 20. His soulbound adviser then begins his formal instruction in spirit magic.

So far this book: Ruwen learns a refinement form and establishes a continuous loop through all twelve meridians. His first spell experiments are dangerously excessive - a cooling attempt freezes Fractal's lake and leaves unstable chaos pockets, one of which destroys parts of his hand. Fractal absorbs the loose spirit before the damage spreads. Ruwen then uses a healing form to regrow his fingers and tests a fire form, burning himself before healing again. These experiments give him working spirit-based healing and fire but show that he still lacks precise control. Meanwhile, Sift deliberately cuts around his own center in the hope that severed meridian paths will reconnect as he heals. The result of his attempt remains uncertain.

So far this book: After healing Sift and the two former enemies, Ruwen meets four armored arrivals driven into Fractal's dungeon. Three are stranded champions of Uru, while the fourth follows Naktos. Micah explains that distortion from Ruwen's core attracts dangerous creatures. The enlarged party enters the mine and encounters a plague siren dreadlord with defenses too strong for conventional attacks. She wounds Hamma and the former hostile mage, tears out Sift's center, and captures Ruwen to drain his spirit. Ruwen reverses the connection and forces his core's power into the siren's already crowded center. With help from his bookworm talisman, he overloads and destroys her center, then harvests the released spirit. The siren is dead, but Sift has suffered a catastrophic wound and needs immediate aid.

So far this book: Ruwen heals Sift and the other wounded survivors, claims protective Scarecrow aspect armor, and helps free a tiny turtle that Sift names Shelly. Condensing the siren's spirit greatly enlarges Ruwen's core. His talisman identifies him as an axiom, a rare person whose center connects to all twelve meridians. The party leaves the mine, reaches the frigid sea, and travels south on an ice platform while Ruwen learns fine essence control. He then spends more than four subjective years in accelerated training, mastering Sift's bamboo and viper step forms while only a day passes outside. An ambush blocks the safe beach near Stone Harbor, forcing the group into its spirit storm. Ruwen discovers his meridians can separate chaotic spirit into its component essences, opens a full harvesting pull to protect everyone, absorbs the entire storm, and collapses.

So far this book: Ruwen wakes after his consciousness and soul were nearly lost in the Stone Harbor storm. Deep in the Unbound lands, he discovers that the Scarecrow armor can project a remote double. Tribunal cultivators associate that aspect with disappearances caused by its previous wearer, and Ruwen adopts the name Reaper rather than submit his core to examination. He invokes single combat and must survive the Sky Clan leader Phoenix for fifteen minutes. Ruwen hides his body, fights through a clone, and uses his newly merged movement form, but stops before crippling Phoenix. He withstands the final inferno with carefully limited harvesting, and the duel ends in a draw that secures passage. Blapy then reveals herself as the ancient Companion, reviews Ruwen's memories, and decides to spare him. She forbids public spirit magic and harvesting, then accepts an alliance with Fractal.

So far this book: Clues purchased from Blapy lead the party to the Oblivion Peaks and the Iris portal valley. Ruwen tells Hamma the truth about his Root class, harvesting, and altered meridians, and she remains his confidante. At the portal lake, the gem-level creature Tickles destroys Ruwen's clone and prevents access to the giant iris flowers. Ruwen drains the lake, sweeps Tickles through a new channel, and seals it outside the valley. He then places iris roots between Sift's center and air meridian. The new paths allow Sift to refine air essence but greatly reduce his sifting ability. Ruwen feeds balanced essence into a closed iris, inscribes a route to Uru's realm, and holds Tickles away with a clone while the party escapes. Everyone reaches the portal, completing the spirit-realm journey.

So far this book: In Uru's realm, the deity returns the older stranded champions to their families and announces an approaching war. She orders Ruwen's core group to restore her third temple, rebuild its lost city, and defend the southern border. Shelly reappears alive in Sift's pocket with evidence that she fought Tickles, and Uru treats the tiny turtle as an important ancient being. The party enters Grey Canyon, where two months have passed and Ruwen's accumulated rewards raise him to level 19 and cultivation stage 40. His meridians and body are heavily fortified, but using spirit publicly would reveal his forbidden aspect. Ruwen identifies valuable portal plants, an Infernal Realm journal, and several soulbound items. He also summons the panther scout Whiskers. Hamma reveals that her missing father once served as Deepwell's high priest, adding another family mystery to the temple mission.

So far this book: Ruwen accepts a future obligation to help the restored shade companion rescue her imprisoned brother, then Whiskers discovers hostile forces near the ruined city. A level-30 bone sculptor controls its skeleton army and blocks the dormant temple. Ruwen, Hamma, Sift, and the shade companion infiltrate with recovered equipment, including Hamma's dangerous bound golem Smash. The bone sculptor traps them, but Ruwen launches the shade companion from his void band onto the mage's rooftop. Smash destroys a rolling skeleton mass and later helps defeat an elite butcher. Ruwen buries the mage in an acid-filled pit and breaks through his bone defenses, while the shade companion lands the critical strike that forces his emergency teleport. The bone sculptor escapes wounded. Inside the temple, the group finds a queue of more than 700,000 dead awaiting revival, including Ruwen's parents and Hamma's father. The restoration system is still inoperable.

Ruwen and Hamma commit to restoring the temple so their families can return. A failsafe says a navigator must supply missing coordinates, and Ruwen discovers the required Architect branches beneath his Root class. He deliberately dies to complete a transfer arranged by Uru. In her realm, Uru explains that Architect is a divine role and charges him with rebuilding the city, raising defenses, and concealing his exceptional potential. Ruwen revives, uses the Navigator branch to generate dimensional and temporal data, and works with his soulbound adviser and the temple intelligence to restart restoration. The guardians awaken and Uru's blessing spreads hundreds of miles. Hamma assigns her father and Ruwen's parents to revival baths, with all three expected back in two days. The bone sculptor remains alive elsewhere. Blapy arrives with Whiskers and other allies and asks where to place a portal to Fractal. Ruwen ends the book responsible for the city, its councils and priesthood, the southern border, the enormous revival queue, and the portal decision.
